text.title: Embedding Generation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every embedding service must enforce.

rule.statement: Same embedding model + same version for both indexing AND querying. Pin in config. Any change = full re-index event.
rule.rationale: Different models live in different vector spaces; cross-model similarity is meaningless.

rule.statement: Always batch. Caps: OpenAI ≤ 2048, Cohere ≤ 96, Voyage ≤ 128. Sort response by `item.index` before mapping back to inputs.
rule.rationale: Single calls 10–50x slower; OpenAI may return out of order.

rule.statement: Cache keys use SHA-256 of (model + version + text). MD5 rejected.
rule.rationale: MD5 collisions at billion-doc scale serve wrong embeddings silently.

rule.statement: Pre-normalize to unit length when cosine intended. Guard against zero-length vectors before normalize.
rule.rationale: Unnormalized cosine equals dot product weighted by magnitude; produces wrong rankings.

rule.statement: Reject or flag: empty strings, whitespace-only, texts > model context.
rule.rationale: Empty texts produce near-zero vectors; overlong texts get silently truncated.

rule.statement: Cohere v3 → pass `input_type` (`search_document` index / `search_query` query). OpenAI text-embedding-3 → `dimensions` parameter for Matryoshka. SentenceTransformer → single instance per process.
rule.rationale: Cohere `input_type` is a 5–10% quality lever; OpenAI dim is a 6.7x storage lever; multi-instance SentenceTransformer OOMs.

rule.statement: New corpus OR rewrite AND vector DB available → wire the embedding service.
Pure keyword search OR low-resource language without multilingual model → skip; use BM25.
